concepts.lesson: Climate refers to the average weather conditions of a large area over a long period of time, usually more than thirty years.
concepts.real_example: India is said to have a monsoon type of climate.

concepts.lesson: Weather refers to the condition of the atmosphere at a particular place and time. It may change from hour to hour or day to day.
concepts.real_example: A rainy day or a sunny afternoon represents weather conditions.

concepts.lesson: The main elements that determine weather and climate include temperature, atmospheric pressure, wind, humidity and precipitation.
concepts.real_example: Heavy rainfall and strong winds during monsoon affect the weather.

concepts.lesson: The climate of India is described as monsoon type because it is mainly influenced by seasonal reversal of winds known as monsoons.
concepts.real_example: Southwest monsoon winds bring rainfall to most parts of India.

concepts.lesson: Several factors influence the climate of a place such as latitude, altitude, pressure and wind system, distance from the sea, ocean currents and relief features.
concepts.real_example: Coastal areas experience moderate climate due to the influence of the sea.

concepts.lesson: Southwest monsoon winds bring heavy rainfall to India during the rainy season. They originate from the southern oceans and move towards the Indian subcontinent.
concepts.real_example: Kerala receives the first monsoon rainfall in early June.
key_points: Two branches: Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal

concepts.lesson: The cold weather season in India occurs from mid-November to February. Temperatures are lower in northern India and the weather is generally dry.
concepts.real_example: Snowfall occurs in the Himalayan regions during winter.

concepts.lesson: The hot weather season lasts from March to May. Temperatures rise significantly and hot winds known as 'loo' blow in northern India.
concepts.real_example: Temperatures may reach 45°C in northwestern India during summer.

concepts.lesson: The advancing monsoon season begins in early June when southwest monsoon winds reach India and bring widespread rainfall.
concepts.real_example: Heavy rainfall occurs along the Western Ghats during this season.

concepts.lesson: The retreating monsoon season occurs during October and November when the southwest monsoon winds withdraw from India.
concepts.real_example: Tamil Nadu receives rainfall during the retreating monsoon season.

concepts.lesson: Rainfall distribution in India varies widely due to relief features, monsoon winds and distance from the sea.
concepts.real_example: Mawsynram in Meghalaya receives the highest rainfall in the world.
key_points: Low rainfall in Rajasthan and Ladakh

concepts.lesson: The monsoon acts as a unifying bond for India because it influences agriculture, water resources, festivals and the overall lifestyle of people across the country.
concepts.real_example: Farmers across India depend on monsoon rainfall for crop cultivation.
